title: Daily Financial Briefing

第一财经 — PBOC Jul 15 briefing
华尔街见闻 — Changxin IPO Jul 16

marketTone: TSMC Q2 beat and raised FY26 revenue >40% confirmed AI demand, but H2 margin compression and BOK's first hike in 3.5 years sent Kospi −6.37%; Williams (Jul 15) said inflation peaked while Jul 15 CPI/PPI both missed and Brent settled $84.95.

summary.text: TSMC Q2 (Jul 16, IR): revenue $40.20bn at top of guide ($39.0–40.2bn), GM 67.7%, EPS $4.31; Q3 guide $44.6–45.8bn; FY26 revenue growth >40% and capex $60–64bn — but management warned H2 gross margins below H1 and ADRs fell ~4% on margin/capex cost fears.

Jul 16 Asia whipsaw: Kospi −6.37% to 6,820.60 (Samsung −8.77%, SK Hynix −11.53%) after Jul 15 +6.24% — BOK raised base rate +25bp to 2.75% (first hike since Jan 2023) and TSMC margin guide landed soft.
Jul 15 US close (Yahoo Finance): S&P +0.38% to 7,572.40, Nasdaq +0.62% to 26,269.23 — second session of gains after June CPI/PPI both missed vs consensus; SOX −2.08% to 12,398.89 (Micron −8.02%).

Williams (Jul 15, NY Fed): inflation 'unquestionably too high' at ~4% but 'encouraging reasons to expect inflation has peaked'; current 3.50%–3.75% stance 'well positioned'; GDP ~2–2¼% and inflation to ~3.25% by year-end — dovish filter after CPI/PPI misses.

China Jul 16: Shanghai −1.85% (broke 3900), Shenzhen −1.97%, ChiNext −2.95% (京报网); Hang Seng +1.33% to ~25,008 (The Standard); PBOC >CNY760bn private relending YTD (Caixin); 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM IPO subscription day (华尔街见闻 3776521).

signal: TSMC confirms AI demand (Q2 $40.2bn, FY26 >40% growth) but H2 margin compression + BOK hike to 2.75% whipsawed Kospi −6.37% after Jul 15 +6.24% — the semi beta is revenue-strong, margin-fragile, and rate-sensitive.
watch: TSMC H2 gross-margin path and $60–64bn capex execution after the Jul 16 revenue beat but ~4% ADR drop — does Korea's −6.37% close mark a crowded-trade unwind or a buying dip?
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%. June CPI and PPI both missed consensus, and Williams (Jul 15) said inflation likely peaked with policy 'well positioned.' Jul 15 US indexes closed higher while SOX fell 2.08%. Brent settled $84.95 and WTI $79.60 as CENTCOM strikes and the Iran port blockade persisted. Jul 16 brought TSMC's record Q2 ($40.20bn) with an H2 margin warning and BOK's first hike since 2023 (+25bp to 2.75%), sending Kospi −6.37%.

globalChanged.text: TSMC Q2 (Jul 16, IR): net revenue $40.20bn (guide $39.0–40.2bn), GM 67.7%, operating margin 60.3%; Q3 revenue guide $44.6–45.8bn, GM 65.0%–67.0%; FY26 revenue growth >40% (prior >30%) and capex $60–64bn (prior $52–56bn); H2 gross margins expected below H1 on 2nm ramp/overseas fabs.
BOK (Jul 16): unanimous +25bp hike to 2.75% from 2.50% — first increase since Jan 2023; cites strong export/investment growth, inflation above target, and financial-stability risks (AP/BOK).
Jul 16 Kospi official close −6.37% to 6,820.60; Samsung −8.77% to 255,000 won; SK Hynix −11.53% to 1,842,000 won — reversing Jul 15 +6.24% gain (Seoul Economic Daily).
Jul 15 US official close (Yahoo Finance): S&P +0.38% to 7,572.40, Nasdaq +0.62% to 26,269.23, Dow +0.29% to 52,658.64; Russell 2000 +0.38% to 2,976.26.
Jul 15 rates/commodities: 10y Treasury 4.55% (Treasury curve, Jul 15); Brent Sep futures settled +$0.22 at $84.95; WTI Aug settled +$0.26 at $79.60 (CNBC).
June PPI miss (BLS, Jul 15): final demand −0.3% m/m (vs unchanged consensus), +5.5% y/y — second straight disinflation print after Jul 14 CPI miss.

Williams (Jul 15, NY Fed): three inflation drivers (tariffs, Middle East energy, AI investment); expects inflation peaked and to decline to ~3.25% by year-end; GDP ~2–2¼%; unemployment to edge to 4% by 2028.

globalImplies.text: TSMC's revenue/capex raise validates AI demand, but H2 margin compression means the market prices profitability, not just top-line — Jul 16 Kospi −6.37% is the cross-asset read-through.
BOK tightening into a semi rally creates a regional rate-differential shock for Korea's leveraged chip positioning — especially after Jul 15 CPI relief lifted Kospi +6.24%.
Williams' Jul 15 dovish frame supports a Jul Fed hold, but Brent $84.95 settle and BOK hike remind that global inflation risks are not uniformly easing.

globalTensions: TSMC revenue beat vs H2 margin warning; CPI/PPI misses vs Brent $84.95; Williams 'inflation peaked' vs BOK hawkish hike; Jul 15 US indices up while SOX −2.08%, then Jul 16 Kospi −6.37% despite AI demand confirmation.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity and structural relending, cautious on price — Jul 15 PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR and SCIO briefing reaffirmed 7-day OMO as main policy rate; Jul 16 Caixin reports >CNY760bn private-sector relending YTD and offshore-yuan market expansion; no LPR-cut signal while oil lifts import-inflation risk.

chinaChanged.text: Jul 16 A-share close (京报网/中新经纬): Shanghai −1.85% (broke 3900), Shenzhen −1.97%, ChiNext −2.95%; STAR 50 −4%+; turnover ~CNY2.42tn; storage/semis weak, pharma/media selective strength.
Jul 16 Hang Seng close +1.33% to ~25,008 (over one-month high); Hang Seng Tech +1.98% — H-shares diverged from onshore weakness (The Standard).
PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR: CNY1.4tn (14000亿元) 6-month operation; CNY900bn maturing → net +CNY500bn (5000亿元) (新华网).

PBOC Jul 15 SCIO briefing (第一财经): Deputy Governor Zou Lan — 7-day OMO remains the main policy rate / market pricing anchor; overnight OMO used for ultra-short liquidity; structural tools can expand if needed.
Caixin (Jul 16): PBOC has channeled >CNY760bn (7600亿元) via private-sector relending and structural programs YTD (Jul 15 SCIO briefing).
Caixin (Jul 16): PBOC outlines offshore-yuan liquidity plan — swap lines, cross-border flows, offshore repo/bond trading to deepen CNH market.
Q2 GDP 4.3% y/y (NBS, Jul 15) vs ~4.5% consensus — weakest since Q4 2022; nominal GDP +5.9% y/y ended 12-quarter deflation streak (Caixin).
Jul 16: 长鑫科技 Changxin DRAM IPO online/offline subscription day (华尔街见闻) — STAR Market issue up to ~76.9亿股 (10% of post-deal share count) per the Jul 9 2026 prospectus/询价 announcement.
Jul 15 Bitcoin (Cointelegraph): BTC/USD reached ~$65,500 intraday — three-week high after the June PPI miss.

chinaImplies.text: Jul 16 onshore tape sold hard (Shanghai −1.85%, broke 3900) despite global AI headlines — domestic growth disappointment and storage/semis weakness still dominate A-share beta while H-shares (+1.33%) diverged.
CNY500bn buyout RR net plus >CNY760bn structural relending shows quantity easing is active, but Zou Lan's rate guidance and oil near $85 argue against an imminent LPR cut.
Changxin IPO and Nvidia H20 resumption (BlockBeats) are supply-chain positives, but Jul 16 A-share semis/PCB still lagged Korea's Jul 15 CPI/semi rip and Jul 16 unwind.

chinaDivergences: Jul 15 Kospi +6.24% vs Shanghai −0.29%; Jul 16 Kospi −6.37% vs Shanghai −1.85% (broke 3900) while Hang Seng +1.33% — Korea's leveraged semi beta whipsaws; onshore weakness vs H-share strength. TSMC revenue beat vs H2 margin warning split AI bulls from margin bears globally.

assetFramework.regime: Fed path via real yields — CPI/PPI relief vs oil/tariff tug; AI earnings as secondary support
assetFramework.driver: Jul 15 close S&P +0.38% / Nasdaq +0.62%; Williams Jul 15 'inflation peaked'; TSMC Q2 beat Jul 16 with margin warning
assetFramework.read: Rates-relief beta held Jul 15; Jul 16 TSMC revenue beat supports AI but margin guide caps enthusiasm — leadership trade, not blanket lift
assetFramework.invalidator: Equities fall while 10y also falls for 2+ sessions — growth fear replacing the rates regime

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs consensus + Fed communication — data-led range
assetFramework.driver: 10y 4.55% Jul 15 (Treasury); CPI/PPI both missed; Williams Jul 15 expects inflation peaked, glide to 3.25% by year-end
assetFramework.read: Disinflation prints pulled yields lower Jul 14–15; Williams' dovish peak-inflation frame supports duration unless oil re-accelerates
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y back above 4.65% without a hot data print — term premium / fiscal regime

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a Hormuz safe-haven bid
assetFramework.driver: CPI/PPI missed Jul 14–15; Brent settled $84.95 Jul 15; BOK hiked +25bp Jul 16
assetFramework.read: Softer Fed path vs oil/geo bid and regional hikes roughly offset — no decisive DXY break verified at Jul 15 close
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while BOK signals further hikes and Brent >$85

assetFramework.regime: Real-yield beta day-to-day with a CB/fiscal-hedge floor
assetFramework.driver: Jul 15 COMEX settle −0.42% at $4,044.00; Jul 14 settle $4,061.10 (+1.60%)
assetFramework.read: Gave back part of the CPI bounce as oil stayed firm — classic tug between real yields and energy shock
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls >3% while real yields decline — hedge-demand floor questioned

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz blockade premium dominates demand signals
assetFramework.driver: Brent Sep settled $84.95 Jul 15 (+$0.22); WTI Aug $79.60; CENTCOM strikes + Iran port blockade (CNBC)
assetFramework.read: Supply-risk beta intact — overwriting June CPI energy relief for forward inflation
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $78 with Hormuz crossings >40/day

assetFramework.regime: Domestic growth/liquidity vs selective global risk beta — A/H split
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai −1.85% Jul 16 (京报网); Hang Seng +1.33%; Jul 15 Q2 GDP 4.3% miss; Changxin IPO subscription Jul 16
assetFramework.read: Onshore sold off hard Jul 16 while H-shares rose — A/H split widened; Changxin IPO + semi weakness weighed A-shares
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 on rising volume with DR007 ≤1.40%

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as policy signal; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.driver: 7-day OMO remains main policy rate (Yicai/Zou Lan); buyout RR net +CNY500bn; >CNY760bn private relending YTD; offshore-yuan expansion plan (Caixin)
assetFramework.read: Stable-fix regime — quantity/structural tools active, LPR pivot not signaled; CNH deepening is internationalization, not depreciation signal
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain
assetFramework.driver: Jul 15 BTC ~$65,500 intraday high on PPI relief (Cointelegraph); Brent settled $84.95
assetFramework.read: Disinflation beta lifted BTC into the mid-$65k area even with Brent near $85 — still rates/liquidity-led, not a safe-haven decouple
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC falls while September hike odds also fall and Brent eases — would break the rates-beta frame

signals.name: TSMC confirms AI demand but H2 margin compression bites
signals.evidence: Q2 revenue $40.20bn at guide ceiling, GM 67.7%; FY26 revenue >40%, capex $60–64bn; H2 GM below H1 warned; ADRs ~−4% (TSMC IR Jul 16).
signals.mechanism: Revenue/capex raise validates AI cycle; margin guide shifts the falsifier from demand to profitability — crowded longs de-risk.
signals.disprovedIf: H2 GM holds ≥67% and Kospi reclaims 7,200 within five sessions.

signals.name: BOK first hike whipsaws Korea semi beta
signals.evidence: BOK +25bp to 2.75% Jul 16 unanimous; Kospi −6.37% (Samsung −8.77%, SK Hynix −11.53%) after Jul 15 +6.24% (BOK/Seoul Economic Daily).
signals.mechanism: Regional tightening into leveraged chip positioning amplifies profit-taking after CPI-relief rally — rate differential shock on top of TSMC margin fears.
signals.disprovedIf: Kospi recovers >50% of Jul 16 loss within three sessions with no further BOK tightening signal.

signals.name: Core disinflation confirmed — CPI and PPI both miss
signals.evidence: CPI core flat m/m / 2.6% y/y; PPI final demand −0.3% m/m / 5.5% y/y (BLS Jul 14–15).
signals.mechanism: Back-to-back downside surprises vs consensus ease near-term re-hike urgency and support duration — unless officials dismiss prints as backward-looking vs oil/tariffs.
signals.disprovedIf: September hike probability reclaims >70% and 10y yields break above 4.65% within one week.

signals.name: Williams dovish peak-inflation frame after soft prints
signals.evidence: Jul 15 speech: inflation ~4% but 'encouraging reasons to expect inflation has peaked'; GDP ~2–2¼%; inflation to ~3.25% by year-end; policy 'well positioned' (NY Fed).
signals.mechanism: NY Fed leadership sets interpretive frame — markets can weigh backward-looking CPI/PPI against forward oil/BOK tightening.
signals.disprovedIf: Multiple Fed speakers explicitly cite tariff goods inflation to keep September hike odds >60%.

signals.name: Hormuz oil premium persists at Jul 15 settle
signals.evidence: Brent settled $84.95 Jul 15; CENTCOM strikes and Iran port blockade ongoing (CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Forward energy inflation risk limits how much June CPI/PPI can reprice the Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $78 for three sessions with Hormuz crossings >40/day.

signals.name: China quantity easing without A-share beta
signals.evidence: PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR Jul 15; >CNY760bn private relending YTD (Caixin); Shanghai −1.85% Jul 16 (京报网).
signals.mechanism: Liquidity lands but Q2 GDP miss keeps onshore growth beta constrained while Korea whipsaws on global semi flows.
signals.disprovedIf: CSI 300 reclaims 4,850 with northbound inflows >CNY5bn for two sessions.

watchItems.headline: TSMC H2 gross-margin path and $60–64bn capex execution
watchItems.why: STRONG TSMC signal — revenue beat confirmed AI but margin guide triggered de-risking.
watchItems.watch: Q3 GM guide 65.0%–67.0%; management H2 below-H1 comment; TSMC ADR vs Jul 16 pre-market −4%.
watchItems.trigger: Further GM guide-down or capex overshoot knocks SOX below 12,200 and Kospi below 6,500.
watchItems.invalidator: Q3 GM prints ≥67% with stable H2 commentary and Kospi reclaims 7,200 within five sessions.

watchItems.headline: BOK tightening path after first hike
watchItems.why: STRONG Korea signal — +25bp Jul 16 whipsawed semis after Jul 15 CPI rally.
watchItems.watch: BOK forward guidance; Kospi vs 6,820.60 Jul 16 close; SK Hynix/Samsung vs Jul 16 lows.
watchItems.trigger: BOK signals another hike within two meetings and Kospi closes below 6,500.
watchItems.invalidator: BOK pauses with inflation forecast cut and Kospi holds 7,000+ for a week.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz / Brent settle band
watchItems.why: MODERATE energy-premium signal that can overwrite June CPI/PPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily settle (Jul 15: $84.95); CENTCOM / Hormuz flow headlines.
watchItems.trigger: Brent settles above $86 for 3 sessions or crossings stay depressed.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $80 with crossings >40/day and no new escalation for 5 days.

watchItems.headline: Williams peak-inflation frame vs BOK/oil
watchItems.why: MODERATE Fed-communication signal after CPI/PPI misses.
watchItems.watch: Fed speakers after Williams Jul 15; 10y vs 4.55% Jul 15; core PCE next print.
watchItems.trigger: Two+ Fed officials cite tariff/oil inflation to keep September hike odds >60%.
watchItems.invalidator: September hike odds fall below 40% with core PCE ≤0.2% m/m.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian — Jul 15–16 semi whipsaw fully priced
watchItems.why: Kospi +6.24% then −6.37% with TSMC revenue beat = crowded positioning stress test.
watchItems.watch: Kospi giving back Jul 15 gain while TSMC FY26 guide holds >40%.
watchItems.trigger: Kospi below 6,700 with TSMC maintaining AI demand commentary = unwind, not regime break.
watchItems.invalidator: Kospi reclaims 7,200 with SOX >12,650 within five sessions.

sources: TSMC IR (Q2 2026), Bank of Korea (Jul 16 decision), NY Fed (Williams Jul 15), Bureau of Labor Statistics (June CPI/PPI archives), Yahoo Finance (Jul 15 US close); AP (BOK), US Treasury yield curve, CNBC (Jul 15 Brent/WTI settle), Yahoo Finance (SOX), NBS (H1/Q2 GDP), 新华网 (PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR), Caixin Global (Jul 15 GDP deflator; Jul 16 private relending/offshore yuan), 第一财经/Yicai (Jul 15 PBOC SCIO briefing), 京报网 (Jul 16 A-share close), The Standard (Hang Seng Jul 16), 华尔街见闻 (Changxin IPO 3776521), BlockBeats (Jul 15 Nvidia H20 flash), Cointelegraph (Jul 15 BTC), GoldSeek/Dow Jones (COMEX gold Jul 15 settle), Seoul Economic Daily (Kospi Jul 16).
singleSource: Rejected 华尔街见闻 3751275 (Williams hawkish ~1pp tariff / GDP ~1%) — Jul 16 2025 article, not 2026 (use NY Fed wil260715). Rejected 华尔街见闻 3751205 (Shanghai −0.03% / Hang Seng −0.29% wrap reused for Jul 16 2026) — wrong-year A/H tape; replaced with 京报网 Shanghai −1.85% and The Standard Hang Seng +1.33%. Rejected BlockBeats 302924 BTC $116,800. Dropped Investing.com TSMC secondary (bot-blocked; TSMC IR is primary). Softened Yicai M2/1.40% prints not present in the fetchable page body.
